title: hints2 Rust Implementation
description: Full Rust port of the hints2 Python application - keyboard-driven GUI navigation tool using hint labels overlaid on screen elements.

stories.title: Workspace Cargo.toml and crate dependency setup
stories.description: Add all required dependencies to workspace and crate Cargo.toml files
acceptanceCriteria: Workspace Cargo.toml has resolver = '2' and lists all 5 member crates
hints/Cargo.toml has: clap (derive), gtk (0.18 GTK3), cairo-rs, gdk, glib, gio, log, env_logger, serde, serde_json, dirs, itertools, zbus
window_systems/Cargo.toml has: serde, serde_json, zbus, x11rb, log
backends/Cargo.toml has: serde, serde_json, log, zbus, atspi, image, screenshots
mouse_service/Cargo.toml has: evdev, tokio (full), serde, serde_json, log, env_logger, glib, gdk, gtk
utils/Cargo.toml has: serde, serde_json, dirs, log, itertools
All crate Cargo.toml files have correct package metadata (name, version, edition = '2021')
cargo check completes (even with placeholder implementations)

stories.title: utils crate: Child, config, errors, hint generation
stories.description: Implement all shared types and utilities in the utils crate
acceptanceCriteria: Child struct has fields: absolute_position: (f64,f64), relative_position: (f64,f64), width: f64, height: f64
Config struct mirrors DEFAULT_CONFIG from Python constants.py with serde Deserialize
load_config() reads ~/.config/hints/config.json and deep-merges with defaults (missing keys fall back to defaults)
get_hints(children: &[Child], alphabet: &str) -> HashMap<String, Child> produces combinatorial labels using itertools::repeat_n product, length = ceil(log(n)/log(|alphabet|))
Error types defined: AccessibleChildrenNotFound, CouldNotFindAccessibleWindow, WindowSystemNotSupported, CouldNotCommunicateWithMouseService - all implement std::error::Error
UNIX_DOMAIN_SOCKET_FILE constant = '/tmp/hints.socket'
MouseButton enum: Left, Right with evdev BTN_LEFT/BTN_RIGHT values
MouseButtonState enum: Down=1, Up=0
MouseMode enum: Move, Scroll
WindowSystemType enum: X11, Wayland

stories.title: window_systems crate: trait and all 5 implementations
stories.description: Implement WindowSystem trait and X11, Sway, Hyprland, Plasmashell, Gnome implementations
acceptanceCriteria: WindowSystem trait has methods: window_system_name()->&str, focused_window_extents()->(i32,i32,i32,i32), focused_window_pid()->u32, focused_application_name()->&str, window_system_type()->WindowSystemType
detect() checks XDG_SESSION_TYPE env var; if wayland, runs 'ps -e -o comm' and matches against {sway, Hyprland, plasmashell, gnome-shell}; returns Box<dyn WindowSystem>
X11 impl: uses x11rb to get active window geometry, PID, and WM_CLASS via _NET_ACTIVE_WINDOW
Sway impl: runs 'swaymsg -t get_tree' + 'swaymsg -t get_workspaces' + 'swaymsg -t get_outputs', parses JSON, computes bar_height = output_height - workspace_height
Hyprland impl: runs 'hyprctl activewindow -j', parses JSON fields at/size/pid/class
Plasmashell impl: uses zbus SessionBus to call org.kde.KWin /Scripting loadScript, runs script, reads journalctl output since start_time, parses JSON
Gnome impl: uses zbus to call uk.co.realh.Hints FocusedWindowInfo DBus method, returns x/y/w/h/pid/name/monitor
get_window_system(override: Option<&str>) -> Result<Box<dyn WindowSystem>, WindowSystemNotSupported> implements full detection logic matching Python get_window_system()

stories.title: backends crate: Backend trait, AT-SPI backend, OpenCV backend
stories.description: Implement the two backends for discovering clickable UI elements
acceptanceCriteria: Backend trait has: get_children(&mut self) -> Result<Vec<Child>, AccessibleChildrenNotFound>, get_application_rules() -> ApplicationRules
ApplicationRules struct holds atspi states/roles/match_types, scale_factor, opencv kernel_size/canny_min_val/canny_max_val
get_application_rules() merges 'default' rules from config with per-application overrides keyed by focused_application_name()
AtspiBackend: uses atspi crate to walk accessibility tree; Collection interface fast-path with MatchRule; recursive fallback when Collection unavailable
AtspiBackend: get_relative_and_absolute_extents() handles Wayland (window-relative coords) vs X11 (screen-absolute coords) and GTK4 negative-x correction
AtspiBackend: get_atspi_active_window() matches by PID from window_system.focused_window_pid(), skips mutter-x11-frames
OpenCV backend: takes screenshot of focused_window_extents using 'screenshots' crate, converts to grayscale, applies Canny edge detection + dilation + findContours via opencv crate
OpenCV backend: applies sway bar_height offset to screenshot region
BackendType enum: Atspi, OpenCV; get_backends(config) returns ordered Vec matching config backends.enable list

stories.title: mouse_service crate: uinput daemon with Unix socket IPC
stories.description: Implement the hintsd mouse service daemon
acceptanceCriteria: Mouse struct creates two evdev UInput devices: relative mouse (EV_REL: REL_X/Y/HWHEEL/WHEEL, EV_KEY: BTN_LEFT/BTN_RIGHT) and absolute mouse (EV_ABS: ABS_X/ABS_Y with screen size max, EV_KEY: BTN_LEFT/BTN_RIGHT)
Mouse::scroll(x,y) writes REL_HWHEEL and REL_WHEEL events
Mouse::move_mouse(x,y,absolute) writes ABS_X/ABS_Y (absolute) or REL_X/REL_Y (relative) then sleeps write_pause
Mouse::click(x,y,button,button_states,repeat,absolute) calls move, iterates button_states writing EV_KEY events, does position-clearing move if absolute
Mouse::do_mouse_action(key_press_state, key, mode) implements sensitivity ramp-up: tracks start_time, increments sensitivity after rampup_time, dispatches left/right/up/down keys from config
MouseService binds Unix socket at /tmp/hints.socket (removing stale file first)
Socket server reads JSON messages with {method, args, kwargs}, dispatches to Mouse methods, writes JSON response
MouseService listens for screen size changes via GDK and recreates Mouse with new dimensions (using total bounding box of all monitors, not just primary)
SIGINT handler closes socket and exits cleanly
Binary named 'hintsd' (not 'mouse_service')

stories.title: hints crate: CLI, mouse IPC client, hint generation orchestration
stories.description: Implement the main hints binary entry point, CLI args, and mouse client
acceptanceCriteria: CLI has: -m/--mode (hint|scroll, default hint), -v/--verbose (count, stackable), -s/--setup (flag)
Verbose flag sets log level: >=1 -> Debug, else Info
Mouse IPC client: send_message(method, args, kwargs) connects to /tmp/hints.socket, sends JSON, reads JSON response
IPC client functions: click(x,y,button,button_states,repeat,absolute), move(x,y,absolute), scroll(x,y), do_mouse_action(key_press_state,key,mode)
hint_mode(config, window_system): iterates enabled backends, calls get_children(), calls get_hints(), displays OverlayWindow, dispatches mouse action (click/hover/grab) via IPC client
scroll_mode(config, window_system): displays InterceptorWindow with action=scroll
get_hints(children, alphabet) -> HashMap<String, Child>: uses itertools product with repeat=ceil(log(n)/log(|alphabet|))
Main entry: loads config, parses CLI args, detects window system (or uses config override), dispatches to hint_mode or scroll_mode or setup

stories.title: hints crate: GTK3 OverlayWindow with Cairo drawing
stories.description: Implement the hint overlay GTK window with full keyboard handling
acceptanceCriteria: OverlayWindow is a GTK3 POPUP window with Cairo-drawn hint labels
Each hint label: yellow background rectangle (configurable RGBA), centered text in configurable font/size/color
Pressed state: overlay hint_selector_state prefix in pressed color over normal color

Keyboard handling: alphabetic keys filter hints (update_hints), digit keys append to repeat count, Escape quits, Ctrl modifier sets hover, Alt modifier sets grab, uppercase key (shift) sets right-click button
When hints filtered to exactly 1: ungrab keyboard, destroy window, set mouse_action with x/y/action/button/repeat
on_show: grab keyboard (X11 only, not Wayland), hide cursor via 'none' cursor name

Window positioned via display_gtk_window: X11 uses move(), Wayland uses GtkLayerShell (set_monitor, set_margin LEFT/TOP, anchor TOP/LEFT, OVERLAY layer, EXCLUSIVE keyboard mode, namespace 'hints'), GNOME uses DBus PositionWindow

InterceptorWindow: 1x1 GTK3 POPUP, key-press -> do_mouse_action (grab->MOVE mode, scroll->SCROLL mode), key-release clears key_press_state, first key press does tiny relative move for Hyprland focus, Escape sends MouseButtonState::Up then quits
on_show for InterceptorWindow: grab keyboard (X11 only)

stories.title: Setup wizard
stories.description: Implement the guided --setup wizard matching Python setup.py
acceptanceCriteria: run_guided_setup() checks for root (uid==0); if not root, prints the sudo command to re-run and exits

setup_accessibility_variables(): appends missing env vars (ACCESSIBILITY_ENABLED, GTK_MODULES, OOO_FORCE_DESKTOP, GNOME_ACCESSIBILITY, QT_ACCESSIBILITY, QT_LINUX_ACCESSIBILITY_ALWAYS_ON) to /etc/environment
setup_uinput_module(): runs 'modprobe uinput' and writes 'uinput' to /etc/modules-load.d/uinput.conf
setup_udev_rules(): writes KERNEL=="uinput" rule to /etc/udev/rules.d/80-hints.rules, adds SUDO_USER to input group via usermod
setup_hintsd(): runs systemctl --machine={USER}@.host --user daemon-reload/enable/start hintsd
setup_gnome_plugin(): symlinks hints extension dir to ~/.local/share/gnome-shell/extensions/
is_wayland_gnome() check adds setup_gnome_plugin to setup list when true
should_continue() prompts user with list of changes before proceeding
show_post_setup_instructions() prompts for reboot after completion

stories.title: cargo build succeeds
stories.description: The entire workspace compiles without errors
acceptanceCriteria: 'cargo build' in project root exits with code 0
No compiler errors in any crate
No unused import warnings that would block compilation
Both binaries are produced: target/debug/hints and target/debug/hintsd
